key_insight: Graph neural networks trained only on finite Cayley graphs can generalize to truncated Cayley graphs of unseen infinite groups, indicating that local graph geometry carries information about group growth.
engineering_relevance: This suggests GNNs may be useful for algebraic pattern recognition and extrapolation in computational group theory, even when only finite surrogates of an infinite object are available.
method: Train a GNN exclusively on finite complete and truncated Cayley graphs.
Evaluate the same model, without retraining, on truncated Cayley graphs from unseen infinite finitely generated groups.
Test across several families including abelian groups, the discrete Heisenberg group, the infinite dihedral group, free groups, and mixed direct products.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong cross-family generalization from finite training graphs to infinite-group truncations, supporting transfer of learned geometric features.
watch_item: Because evaluation uses truncated Cayley graphs, performance may depend on truncation scale and may not fully capture global properties of the infinite groups.
paper_title: From Finite Cayley Graphs to Growth of Infinite Groups
